other_observers: Nick Komar, Andy Boyce, and Dave Leatherman; first found by Joey Kellner, and seen by Glenn Walbek, Joe Roller, Norm Erthal, and Allison Hilf
description: the bird was a medium sized warbler, about the same size as nearby Black-and-white Warblers, and a little larger than nearby Wilson's Warblers. The bill was black with some pinkish coloration towards the base. The legs were a dusky pinkish color.  The forecrown of the bird was a dusky olive-greenish color. The remainder of the crown, face, nape, back, wings, and tail were all a clean slate-gray color. The bird had a bold yellow supraloral and a bold white eyering. The malar was gray with a few black feathers mixed in. The underparts were bright yellow, with the exception of the white undertail coverts. There was a necklace of dusky gray streaks on the chest. These were noticeable, but not particularly bold.Behaviors: the bird was mostly by itself, though occasionally it seemed to be in the company of two Black-and-white Warblers or a few Wilson's Warblers. It foraged at mid to upper levels in the trees, but once came down to below eye level.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic female or first basic male
